posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked: Blue fruits, purple flowers and menthol. Full and fleshy, creamy mid-palate. Layered and luscious, nice energy and balance. Utterly delicious and plenty of material upon which to age.

posts.name: Phil T r o t t i e r
posts.cooked: I had this tonight with a turkey feuilleté and it’s delicious! Very expressive nose of ripe berries, spices, a touch of oak and some delicate floral notes. It took a while before I stopped inhaling those aromas and actually took a sip. It’s pretty big: mouthful of fruit, nice concentration from entry to finish. But as Jeremy pointed out, energy and balance ring true. There is plenty of acidity, tanins and slight bitterness to keep everything in check and make it very approachable right now. That being said, my others will lay low for a while as this should age very nicely.
posts.display_username: Phil T r o t t i e r

always good to see a dujac note from you. happy xmas and new year.

i reckon the market is underestimating 18s in general

The market in Quebec, Canada is way short in supply for 2018 burgundy ( despite SAQ is operating by the provincial Government ) and the demand is high. They are flying off their shelves and one needs to act fast to get hold of them.

posts.name: Phil T r o t t i e r
posts.cooked:
Phil…beautiful wine glass.

Sadly for some unknown reasons, very seldom get into MStDenis and Dujac…

Glasvin Aromatic. I’m quite happy with them.

As for all Dujac in QC, I know why I seldom get into them… AVAILABILITY! Having to win the lottery to get bottles is such a pain.

I got all my Burgundy glasses - Hong Kong Hip : By Lucaris and Spiegelau - Salute Verre a Bourgogne Copa de Borgona - from Bowring, Montreal, Quebec. BUT…they are not hand-made.

Hong Kong Hip (burgundy balloon) Lucaris : w-122 h-251 mm; capacity 0.95 l.

Spiegelau - Salute - Verre a Bougogne Copa de Borgona - w-108 mm=4.25 inch; h-247 mm =9.7 inch; V-810 ml =28.4 once.
